question_html:
Write down the appropriate preposition for the following sentences:

(a) The nurse waited _____ the patient.

(b) We are related _____ marriage.

(c) He is vulnerable _____ criticism.

(d) We should not yield _____ any pressure.

(e) The passport will be valid _____ one year.
solution_html:
(a) on/for (b) to/by (c) to (d) to (e) for

Marks Distribution:

For writing the appropriate prepositions; (0.5×5)=2.5 marks.

question_html:
Make sentences with the following group verbs:

look after, call off, carry out, set up, put off.
solution_html:
(a) She promised to look after my cat while I am away.
‎(b) They had to call off the picnic because of heavy rain.
‎(c) The scientist will carry out a new experiment next week.
‎(d) We plan to set up a community library in our area.
‎(e) The meeting was put off until next Monday.

Marks Distribution:
‎(0.5×5) = 2.5 marks for making sentences with group verbs.

question_html: Write a paragraph on “Food Safety”.
solution_html:
Food Safety

Food Safety indicates a safe situation in respect of food, its uninterrupted supply and availability of adequate food materials throughout the year. Food safety has primarily two aspects, namely family or household food security and national food security. Household food security depends on the ability of the household wage earners to secure enough food to ensure an adequate dietary intake of all of its members at all times for a healthy and active life. Similarly, national food security depends on the ability of the government to secure enough food for the whole nation. To achieve food security three things, need to be highlighted; first, availability of adequate food for the family and the nation; second, reasonable stability in the supply of food spatially and seasonally; and third, physical, social and economic access of a household to sufficient, safe and good quality food. In Bangladesh population groups who are most vulnerable to household food insecurity include landless farmers, day labourers, households headed by destitute women, and people living in disaster prone areas like chars, islands, and refugee camps. From the review of the results obtained in the nutrition surveys so far done, it appears that almost 90% of the population of the country suffer from some of nutritional deficiencies due to the lack of adequate food intake.

Marks Distribution:

For writing Title, “Food Safety”; 0.5 mark.

For mentioning and describing the aspects of food security and the factors that determine food security; 1.5 marks.

For writing the key components of food security; 1.5 marks.

For mentioning vulnerable groups in Bangladesh who are most insecure about food safety; 1.5 marks.

[Instructions:

* Students must write it within one single para. If it becomes more than one para, 50% marks have to be deducted from the obtained marks.

* 0.5 mark will be deducted for one or two grammar or spelling mistakes, 01 mark for three or more grammar or spelling mistakes.]
